steps.title: Rewrite the inequality
steps.content:
$3x - 7y > 21$. Solve for $y$: $-7y > 21 - 3x \implies y < \frac{3x - 21}{7} = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$.

The solution region is below the line $y = \frac{3}{7}x - 3$ (which has positive slope and y-intercept $-3$).

steps.title: Test each region
steps.content:
Quadrant II ($x<0, y>0$): pick $(-1, 1)$. $3(-1)-7(1) = -10$, not $> 21$. So Q2 has no solutions — choice A.

Q3 ($x<0, y<0$): pick $(-1,-10)$. $3(-1)-7(-10)=67>21$. Q4 ($x>0,y<0$) clearly works. The x-axis ($y=0$): $3x>21\implies x>7$ — solutions exist.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
